what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML render proof that loads shared styles and runtime scripts, fetches layout data, creates stub board rows, converts them into a board face record grouped by type, renders the board-view archetype in portrait orientation, and mounts it into the page.
observations: The page title identifies it as a board-view render proof for breadth #2.
It loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
It uses absolute paths for shared CSS, runtime scripts, and /dna/layouts.json.
The inline dataset contains guide, decision, and note rows with title, state, channel, and address fields.
The board record is built with groupBy:"type".
The rendered archetype receives a visualDevice generated from board groups at width 760.
The script appends the rendered card to #mount after asynchronous layout loading.
standouts: The file is a compact end-to-end fixture from stub board data to rendered archetype output.
The inline comment frames the data as a raw /api/board-shape stub used to prove adapter plus render behavior.
The visual device is supplied separately from the record, keeping data shape and board-group visualization distinct.
conventions: Uses a minimal mount-point HTML shell with runtime-driven rendering.
Keeps fixture data inline inside the proof file.
Relies on the global DNA namespace supplied by prior script tags.
Uses design vocabulary such as archetype, faceRecord, visualDevice, substrate, channel, and address.
Uses absolute project-root asset paths rather than relative file paths.
concerns: No error handling is present for the layouts fetch or render path.
The proof depends on global script load order and a global DNA object.
Absolute asset paths require a server context that serves /surface and /dna.
The embedded stub rows make the proof static unless edited by hand.
notes: A file-local board-view proof that exercises the adapter-to-archetype render path with representative board rows.
questions: What exact structure is expected inside layouts.archetypes["board-view"]?
What should happen if /dna/layouts.json fails to load?
Is width 760 a fixed proof value or tied to an intended device size?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated render-proof purpose matches the file: it builds a stub board record and renders the board-view archetype into the mount element.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate that board-shaped data can be adapted into the design system's face record format and rendered through the board-view archetype with a grouped visual treatment.
novelty: The file combines a board-shape API stub, archetype rendering, and a separate organism-generated visual device in a small standalone proof page.
contribution: Contributes a concrete board-view fixture showing how ledger-like rows with type, state, channel, and address fields are grouped and passed into the render system with a matching visual device.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone board-view HTML proof that loads design runtimes, fetches layout definitions, adapts inline board rows into a grouped record, renders the board-view archetype, and mounts it into the page.
intention_for_embedding: Created to verify the adapter and render path for board-shaped substrate data using a compact static fixture.
